items.title: Top spy boss first witness as antisemitism royal commission resumes

children.content: The head of Australia's spy agency will be the first witness when the royal commission on antisemitism resumes this week.

gallery.altText: Director-General of the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation (ASIO) Mike Burgess

thumbnail.altText: Director-General of the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation (ASIO) Mike Burgess

items.title: 'I've had enough': Why this loyal Labor voter is switching to One Nation

children.content: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a purple t shirt

items.title: Man charged after 14yo restaurant worker threatened with knife

children.content: Police say a 30-year-old man who allegedly demanded money from a 14-year-old worker at knifepoint, on the NSW Central Coast, was tackled to the ground by two other men.

image.altText: A chinese restaurant from the road with a car driving past.

items.title: Pipes, posters and packaging show Australia's history of smoking

children.content: Smoking artefacts from as far back as the early 1900s show the Far North Coast how accepted smoking once was.

gallery.altText: A man smiling and wearing glasses looks through a scrapbook
tobacco tins lines up on a table
An older scrapbook with small war postcards in it.

thumbnail.altText: A man smiling and wearing glasses looks through a scrapbook
tobacco tins lines up on a table
An older scrapbook with small war postcards in it.

items.title: From farm gate to stage door, theatre is a release from life on the land

children.content: Putting their livestock, tractors and drought feeding aside for a night, these farmers are taking to the stage.

image.altText: Man with akubra looks at camera, pink sky and field in background.

items.title: My dog Teddy was more than just a pet. We needed each other

children.content: Judith Geppert has an impressive resume of achievements and celebrations, but her capabilities are often questioned. Her dog Teddy, however, never treated her any differently.

image.altText: Judy pats her dog Teddy while sitting in her wheelchair

items.title: Endangered turtle found to have survived record-breaking flood

children.content: Experts are relieved to see the number of endangered Manning River turtles holding steady, a year after devastating flooding in northern New South Wales.

image.altText: Underwater image of a turltle approaching a fish laid as bait to measure numbers

gallery.altText: A small turtle with a yellow stripe along its face and side, in a person's hand.

thumbnail.altText: A small turtle with a yellow stripe along its face and side, in a person's hand.

items.title: North Queensland crowd raises record figure for Arrow and MND charity

children.content: North Queensland climbs into the NRL's top six with an impressive 30-18 victory over South Sydney in Townsville, where fans raise thousands of dollars for the Fight MND charity.

image.altText: Jai Arrow looks on during a South Sydney game

items.title: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic

children.content: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Bulldogs get home in a see-sawing contest against the Demons.

image.altText: Toby Greene celebrates a goal against the Lions

items.title: Former actor Rachel Ward became a farmhand at 65 and found her purpose

children.content: With the country wind in her grey hair, cow dung on her boots and not a skerrick of make-up, former actor Rachel Ward has found her place, sharing her passion for regenerative farming.

gallery.altText: Rachel Ward with her Andy Warhol painting.
Woman with short grey hair wears glasses, long pants, buttoned blouse holding to the handles, standing on side of blue tractor
Actor and director Rachel Ward in Cosmo magazine.

thumbnail.altText: Rachel Ward with her Andy Warhol painting.

items.title: Police investigate triple fatal crash in Sydney's south

children.content: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, the ABC understands one of the vehicles was likely a rideshare car.

items.title: Charges withdrawn against artist for displaying Nazi symbols in artwork

children.content: The case has been dropped against an Australian artist charged with displaying Nazi symbols on an artwork depicting politicians and billionaires.

image.altText: A man in front of a red poster with the word stop stares ahead, he has a white goatee and salt and pepper hair

items.title: IVF inquiry hears harrowing stories of fertility treatment outside big cities

children.content: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.

image.altText: A brown haired woman with bright pink glasses stands in front of a wooden door looking seriously into camera

items.title: Auckland makes A-League history with grand-final triumph over Sydney

children.content: Auckland has become the first side from New Zealand to win an A-League Men championship after downing competition heavyweight Sydney 1-0 in a cagey season decider.

image.altText: A soccer player in blue and black screams in celebration with floodlight behind him

items.title: ASIO holiday warning did not flag elevated Hanukkah threat

children.content: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.

image.altText: People hug at floral tribute Bondi Beach promenade

items.title: Plastic waste collected in Sydney Harbour during Vivid festival rising

children.content: Data from floating rubbish bins near the Sydney CBD suggests there are higher levels of rubbish entering the water when major events such as Vivid are on.

gallery.altText: Plastic utensils, brightly coloured wrappers and straws and other odd shapes of plastic spaced out on white background.
A wide landscape shot at sunrise of five kayakers under the Sydney Harbour Bridge with the Opera House and clouds backdrop.
A circular void in flat water with a range of plastic bottles and other floating rubbish about to be sucked in.

thumbnail.altText: Plastic utensils, brightly coloured wrappers and straws and other odd shapes of plastic spaced out on white background.
A wide landscape shot at sunrise of five kayakers under the Sydney Harbour Bridge with the Opera House and clouds backdrop.
A circular void in flat water with a range of plastic bottles and other floating rubbish about to be sucked in.

avatar.alt: A man in a blue checked shirt and blue jacket with a beard and long hair.

items.title: Dangerous area in Sydney for rock fishers trials life jacket library

children.content: Rock fishers in Sydney's east can now borrow and return life jackets for free in a bid to help reduce the number of drownings in the area.

image.altText: the back of a man's head wearing a life jacket as he looks over the ocean at nearby rocks

items.title: Mental health evidence delays sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's murderer

children.content: The defence team for Daniel Billings has submitted evidence from a forensic psychiatrist that found he was in an "abnormal state of mind" when he killed his former partner.

items.title: 'Pink lady' denies drafting her own job description before council interview

children.content: The commission was shown a text to the former chief executive of Parramatta Council from Roxanne Thornton featuring a handwritten note detailing her salary and entitlements.

image.altText: Woman with partly shaved andd blonde hair, black suit jackets and green glasses

items.title: Solution arrives for crash-prone 'Russian roulette' intersection

children.content: After almost a decade of campaigning, residents welcome safety improvements at a notorious stretch of the Princes Highway considered the most crash-prone turn-off in southern New South Wales.

items.title: Police probing 70s cold cases to investigate links to knife, shovel and dress at campsite

children.content: Police will review the testimony of a former youth camp manager who found a number of suspicious items in bushland around the time of multiple disappearances near Newcastle in the 1970s.

items.title: Three men charged after police discover alleged bomb-making lab

children.content: Three men have been charged with explosives and firearms offences as specialist police teams search a home in a quiet regional street.

image.altText: A Hazardous Materials Unit and fire truck parked on a street, with a red HAZMAT tent set up on grass.
